code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        # more natural phrasing for people
        "genre": "is a {obj} musician",
        "instrument": "plays {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is a member of the band {obj}",
        "recordLabel": "is signed to {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started their career in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended their career in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "originated from {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "is a fusion genre of {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "is a subgenre of {obj}",
        "derivative": "is a derivative of {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        # leader is handled specially to place the object as the subject.
        "leader": "SPECIAL_LEADER",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "belongs to the country {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        # special handling for demonym to produce a natural sentence
        "demonym": "SPECIAL_DEMONYM",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "background": "has a background as {obj}",
    }

    # Group objects by (subject, predicate) to allow proper pluralisation.
    from collections import defaultdict

    # Helper to format a list of objects in natural language.
    def _fmt_list(items: list[str]) -> str:
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        if len(items) == 2:
            return f"{items[0]} and {items[1]}"
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + ", and " + items[-1]

    # Templates for singular and plural forms.
    singular_map = pred_map.copy()
    plural_map = {
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "are associated with {obj}",
        "genre": "are {obj} musicians",
        "instrument": "play {obj}",
        "occupation": "are {obj}",
        "nationality": "are {obj}",
        "background": "have backgrounds as {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "were born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originate from {obj}",
        # default fallback for any predicate with multiple values
    }

    # Collect objects per (subject, predicate)
    subj_pred_objs: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    # Also collect simple location information for later embedding (e.g., label locations)
    location_dict: dict[str, str] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        if pred == "location":
            # store location of the subject entity
            location_dict[subj] = obj
        else:
            subj_pred_objs[(subj, pred)].append(obj)

    # Build clauses per subject
    subject_clauses: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for (subj, pred), objs in subj_pred_objs.items():
        objs_formatted = _fmt_list(objs)
        # Special handling for the 'derivative' predicate: object is a derivative of the subject.
        if pred == "leader":
            # For leader, the object is the actual person; make it the subject of the clause.
            clause = f"{objs_formatted} is the leader of {subj}"
        elif pred == "derivative":
            # When there are multiple objects, create separate clauses for each.
            if len(objs) == 1:
                clause = f"{objs_formatted} is a derivative of {subj}"
            else:
                # join multiple derivative objects with commas and 'and'
                clause = f"{_fmt_list(objs)} are derivatives of {subj}"
        else:
            # Special handling for recordLabel to embed location if known
            if pred == "recordLabel" and len(objs) > 0:
                objs_with_loc = []
                for o in objs:
                    if o in location_dict:
                        objs_with_loc.append(f"{o} (in {location_dict[o]})")
                    else:
                        objs_with_loc.append(o)
                objs_formatted = _fmt_list(objs_with_loc)
            # Choose appropriate template
            if len(objs) > 1 and pred in plural_map:
                template = plural_map[pred]
            else:
                template = singular_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {objs_formatted}")
            clause = template.format(obj=objs_formatted) if "{obj}" in template else template
        subject_clauses[subj].append(clause)

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, clauses in subject_clauses.items():
        # Join clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last clause.
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            body = clauses[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
